double_count_risk: medium: Helios deducted; customer-site overlap with Microsoft/OpenAI/Meta Epoch sites is candidate-only. PF1 (~400 MW) handled separately as Applied Digital leaseback.
notes: Rev-4.3: 3.1 GW total contracted (CoreWeave 10-K) includes 850 MW active fleet AND 800 MW Helios buildout (already in Epoch). Net incremental ex-Epoch = 3.1 - 0.8 = 2.3 GW. This atom captures the full CoreWeave non-Epoch contracted footprint (active + future); coreweave_operational_disclosed is excluded as a subset to avoid double-counting (reviewer #5 fix).

site_or_region: Hyperion stretch beyond Epoch evidenced 2.262 GW
tier_rationale: T5 because the 5 GW figure is source-disclosed CEO statement and Engineering blog corroborates, but lacks: (a) named permits beyond LPSC U-37425 which supports ~2 GW initial generation; (b) firm site-plan beyond initial Hyperion campus; (c) hardware/financing JV expansion past current $27B Blue Owl envelope. Not T4 because no permitting/utility/JV amendment ties the stretch to specific additional MW. Not T6 because the GW figure is direct CEO disclosure.
tier_rationale_quote: "can scale up to 5GW over several years" — Zuckerberg Q2 2025
notes: Rev-4.3 source replacement (reviewer #7 fix): replaced local cached transcript URL with publicly hosted Meta Q2 2025 transcript PDF. Stretch = 5.0 GW Zuckerberg statement minus 2.262 GW Epoch Hyperion buildout floor. Engineering at Meta blog (2025-09-29) corroborates "scale up to 5 GW once finished". Meta FY2025 10-K corroborates Hyperion JV with $27B development cost and four-year initial lease (2029).

site_or_region: "vast majority" US-sited per Anthropic; physical shells unspecified (Google-owned campuses, Fluidstack TX/NY shells, or operational/financial partners per Broadcom 8-K)
tier_rationale: T5 because all 3-of-3 source-content checks pass (counterparty: Google Cloud + Broadcom; magnitude: 4.5 GW IT total; window: 2026-2031), but no specific physical site is named. Anthropic explicitly says "vast majority will be sited in the United States" but does not name campuses. Not T4 because no named site/project. Not T6 because the GW figure is source-disclosed, not analyst-inferred. Per user direction, this row is now visible in the paper at T5 with explicit dedupe range, replacing the prior Class B chip-procurement zero treatment that violated the 3-of-3 inclusion test.
tier_rationale_quote: "approximately 3.5 gigawatts" (Broadcom 8-K) + "vast majority will be sited in the United States" (Anthropic Apr 6 2026) — counterparty + magnitude + broad window present, named site absent.
double_count_risk: high (partially deduped Rev-4.3): central case removes 2.7 GW assumed Epoch overlap (Fluidstack 0.509 + 50% of Google Epoch sites); range 0-5.4 GW spans full overlap to no overlap.
notes: Rev-4.3 NEW ATOM (per user direction: "Anthropic-Google deal should exist in the paper at appropriate tier"). Total Anthropic-Google/Broadcom TPU exposure = 1 GW IT (Anthropic Oct 23, 2025) + 3.5 GW IT (Broadcom 8-K Apr 6, 2026) = 4.5 GW IT. Central case assumes 50% physical-shell overlap with existing Google Epoch sites (Goodnight 1.09 GW + New Albany 0.54 + Cedar Rapids 0.63 + Council Bluffs E 0.28 + Pryor N 0.45 + Omaha 0.47 = 3.46 GW Google candidates) plus Fluidstack Lake Mariner (0.51 GW). Net-new central = 5.4 GW facility / 2 = 2.7 GW. Low = 0 (full overlap absorbed by Epoch + Fluidstack); high = 5.4 GW (no overlap, full net-new physical capacity).

tier_rationale_quote: "up to one gigawatt of compute capacity" with no named sites
double_count_risk: high (partially deduped Rev-4.3): central case applies 50% Fairwater overlap assumption (590 MW of 1180 MW removed); low case = 0 incremental (full Fairwater carve-out), high case = 1180 MW (full net-new).
notes: Rev-4.3 dedupe adjudication: Microsoft and NVIDIA both confirm Vera Rubin (the chip class Anthropic uses on Azure) deploys at Fairwater Wisconsin and Atlanta. Central case applies a 50% Fairwater overlap assumption (590 MW facility net-new), with low=0 (full overlap, all Anthropic Azure capacity carved from Fairwater Epoch rows) and high=1180 (no overlap, Anthropic gets net-new Microsoft sites). Documented as judgmental; sources do not disclose Anthropic site list.
